description: Figure 1 displays the regression result for ferrite apparent density, defined as the inverse of the regression coefficient r_i with 95% probability error bars, compared against pure elemental densities and literature average values for alloying elements in ferritic and low-alloy steels. The horizontal bars represent the regression apparent densities, open squares denote pure elemental densities, and open triangles indicate literature averages. Data are shown for Fe, Si, Mn, Cr, Mo, Ni, V, Cu, and S. The regression model (R² = 0.994) demonstrates that Cu and Mo increase ferrite density, while C, Cr, Mn, Ni, S, Si, and V reduce it. Good agreement exists between regression and literature values for Cr, Cu, Fe, Mn, Ni, S, and Si, with some deviation for Mo and notable deviation for V. Elemental densities align with regression results for Cr, Cu, Fe, Mn, Mo, and S, but deviations occur for Ni, Si, and V, indicating non-ideal solution effects. This validates the regression approach for ferrite density prediction.
